sources.notes: DOCX text is explanation fragments rather than complete stems/options. No question objects were emitted because answer keys would be inferred, not sourced.
sampleFragments: Prima e command ca order ul este procesat si asteapta in queue pana ajunge si e considerat completat ( the order will be delivered in the future may be a hint )
A doua e decorator pleaca de la o baza comuna ( orders ) si adauga filters/rules in the future . Nu e strategy nu mentioneaza ca (,) clientul alege ce se intampla cu comand a.
Prima state sunt mentionate mai multe state uri pentru equipment care se updateaza automat fara interventia noastra in functie de in ce stare se afla
A doua e factory ( family of classes ; generate different types of analysis being decoupled from the details of creating these types ) si ultima propozitie cu over time e hint
Prima command : requests, saves in the queue

Ca sa fie singleton ne trb getInstance , ar fi eager si ne ar trebui si instanta sa fie private => un raspuns ar fi ca f : it cant be considered an implementation of a design pattern dar boja a facut un ex fix asa
Prima flyweight zice de RAM si the courses folloed by students are common not clones ( prototype)
A doua strategy ( strategia este aleasa de client = teacher at runtime)

Diagrama pentru command se poate vedea in callbacktwo ca sunt toate informatiile de care are nevoie un obiect ca sa execute un task ( asta face command : transforma o actiune intr un obiect de sine statator)
Prima composite : tree-like structure , ultima propozitie din primu paragraph

verificationNeeded.prompt: Considering the fact that the power method raises a number to a certain power, the following unit test type is (choose one from the next CORRECT or Right-BICEP options):

The following scenario is considered: A person can apply for the change of driver's license only at the special dedicated counter. From here the request will be analyzed by the duty officer who will ask the printer to print a new permit if this is legal. From the printing office, the permit will arrive at the pick-up counter, where it can be picked up by the applicant. The method of evaluating the possibility of issuing a new permit, as well as all the stages it goes through from the submission of the application to the issuance, are not transparent for the applicant. What combination of Design Patterns can you use to implement this scenario?
How many unit tests will be marked as successfully (green check icon) considering the next test case?
The 70/20/0 rule refers to
Which of the following patterns (select one or more options) implements the callback principle, decoupling the module you manage from the actual implementation of the action?
Associate each Design Pattern from the list below with the problem it solves:
Which of the following statements about the Cross-Check Testing Principle from Right-BICEP are correct
Choose the annotations used in JUnit 4 for the following elements:

You are doing a code review for a colleague. What are the Clean Code rules/principles that must be implemented in the code and from the perspective of which you can consider that the solution is clean. Select one or more answers.
Which of the following Design patterns are also considered Wrappers for the original classes?

Develop a software solution for a car wash. Within it, the process of washing the car involves going through some steps in a well-established order: adding degreaser, washing, adding foam, rinsing, wiping. This process is similar for all laundromats. The solution must be modified so that it only starts if there is enough degreaser and enough detergent to foam. Otherwise, the car wash process will not start, waiting for the tanks to be filled. What are the 2 design patterns that optimally solve these 2 problems?
Select from the code examples below only the variant(s) that correctly implement the "singleton" design pattern:
Associate the situations described below with the related Right-BICEP principles.
Match the variables with the used naming standard:

You develop an application that allows electronic payment of invoices. What design patterns should you implement to optimally solve the following requirements (select one or more patterns)? payment can be made using any existing processor, such as Visa, Mastercard, etc. The module that executes the payment is implemented independently of the processor. The processor is indicated when the bank card data is entered. The solution must allow the flexible addition of new processors in the future the application must allow additional operations to be carried out before the payment reaches the processor. The solution must allow the addition of these additional operations (verifications, validations, data analysis, etc.) without affecting the main payment execution flow the application can allow the user to schedule payments for a future date so that they are not made instantly.

It is considered the Composite class from the implementation of the design pattern with the same name. Match the terms below according to the correct implementation of this pattern. public class Composite implements blank Composite { blank List <Component> components = new blank ArrayList <Component>(); public void remove(Component component) { components.remove(component); } public void add(Component blank component ) { components.add(component); } }
The following method and related tests are given. Which of the unit tests fails?
Select the differences between the State and Strategy patterns
Determine for each design pattern which category it belongs to:

It is considered the Composite class from the implementation of the design pattern with the same name. Match the terms below according to the correct implementation of this pattern. public class Composite implements blank { blank <Component> components = new blank <Component>(); public void remove(Component component) { components.remove(component); } public void add(Component blank ) { components.add(component); } }

You implement the Product class that contains the quantity attribute. The attribute accepts values between 5 and 100 (including these 2 values). To allow clients to change the Product quantity you also implement the setQuantity(int value) method. The method should validate the input value. If you implement a unit test, what values should you use as input for setQuantity() for a RANGE test ? Choose one ore more values

You have created a software application that allows you to broadcast live sports events on different platforms. In the future, you want to be able to add different features to this broadcast dynamically, such as Chat Feature (allows viewers to chat during the broadcast), Viewer Statistics (displays the number of viewers and other statistics in real-time), Advertisements: (inserts advertisements at regular intervals), etc. Choose the proper design pattern and implement a solution that will allow you to extend the collection of features without changing the existing solution. Also, multiple features can be applied to the same initial classic broadcast. The streaming application allows users to issue various actions, such as sending a chat message to another user, start recording the current broadcast, change stream quality/resolution. Each of these operations will be executed asynchronously and will not stop the current live broadcast stream. Choose the proper design pattern and implement a solution that will allow the system to manage a queue of user actions and to be able to process new actions in the future. Select one or more design patterns that will help you implement previous requirements in an efficient manner.
What annotations from the following do you use within your JUnit Test class in JUnit 4?
Select the correct statement (one ore more) about a design pattern
Între'inere, camer video

questions.prompt: In the following figure, the implementation of a Singleton Registry was needed. Identify issues within this Singleton Registry implementation:

The getInstanta() method must be final, but in the implementation it is static
The static function getInstanta() must return a single instance, but in the implementation it returns a collection
The static function getInstanta() must add the new product to the collection only if it does not exist, but the implementation adds it if it exists.
The constructor of the class must be public, but in the implementation it is private
answer: The static function getInstanta() must return a single instance, but in the implementation it returns a collection
The static function getInstanta() must add the new product to the collection only if it does not exist, but the implementation adds it if it exists.

questions.prompt: Which of the following statements about the Cross-Check Testing Principle from Right-BICEP are correct
options: It allows the use of parameterized tests to check the consistency of the results.
Performs validation of test results by comparing them with results obtained by an external reference system.
Validates the results of a test with predefined values.
Cross-Check testing involves checking the results of a method using other independent methods.
Reduce necesitatea de a scrie documentație detaliată pentru teste.
It involves ensuring that a test covers all branches of the code under test
Cross-Check testing allows different algorithms to be used to calculate the same result.

questions.prompt: The Singleton design pattern is used when:
options: an object is created based on another object
an object from a class framework is created
an intermediate object is created compared to the original object
a unique object of a class is created
a verification object of the original object is created
a single instance of a class is needed
answer: a unique object of a class is created
a single instance of a class is needed
questions.explanation: Visible selected answer received full marks in the Moodle review.

questions.prompt: You are doing a code review for a colleague. What are the Clean Code rules/principles that must be implemented in the code and from the perspective of which you can consider that the solution is clean. Select one or more answers.
options: The code must contain comments that explain how the function is implemented
Functions must return null as a result, if it does not exist or cannot be determined
The functions must validate the value of the input parameters
Functions must NOT return null as a result if it does not exist
The source code of a function must fit on a screen (minimum 14 inches)
Classes must contain accessor methods at the expense of public attributes
Functions must throw exceptions if they cannot process the data
Use generic names for variables and functions
It uses names of variables and functions that are related to the domain of the solved problem
Use variable and function names that reveal intent
Functions should be small (as number of lines of source code)
Classes must be small (in number of lines of source code)
The functions must return error codes if they cannot process the data
questions.explanation: The selected option was visible, but the Moodle grade was zero; it is evidence of an attempt, not an answer key.

questions.prompt: In the restaurant, customers have the opportunity to choose one of the products available in its menu. However, over time, it has been noticed that there are additional requests for a selection of products with Spanish and Italian specifics. Thus, for each product available in the restaurant's menu, when it is prepared, an additional topping related to the selected specifics can be added. The selection of the culinary specialty does not lead to a change in the basic price of the dish. Customers can also order basic items from the menu, without having to make any changes to the basic recipe. What is the design pattern that shapes the existing problem from the statement?

questions.prompt: Using the Proxy design pattern is recommended when:
options: It is desired to add new attributes to an existing class
It is desired to create a unique object that will perform the action
It is desired to adapt some existing functionalities to new contexts
It is desired to restrict/control the call of an existing functionality
It is desired to simplify the calling of an existing functionality
answer: It is desired to restrict/control the call of an existing functionality
questions.explanation: Visible selected answer received full marks in the Moodle review.

questions.prompt: Which of the following statements about the Adapter design pattern are true:
options: It can be implemented using composition (HAS-A) or inheritance (IS-A).
It allows using a wrapper class to convert an old interface to a new one.
Allows extending a class to add new functionality.
It allows adding new functionality to an existing class without modifying it.
Allows incompatible interfaces to be used together as one.
Allows objects to change their behavior at runtime.
answer: It can be implemented using composition (HAS-A) or inheritance (IS-A).
It allows using a wrapper class to convert an old interface to a new one.
Allows incompatible interfaces to be used together as one.
questions.explanation: Visible selected answer received full marks in the Moodle review.

questions.prompt: It has been observed that when the internet connection is weak, the loading of the resources of a web page takes a very long time. Thus, the decision was made to optimize this process by creating a filter on the loading elements. A web page is represented by a list of Item type objects (paragraphs, images, etc.). Filtering consists of several stages such as: removing image objects, changing paragraph settings by reducing them to black only, without other settings such as bold, italics, etc. It is recognized that these steps can be interchanged and also that additional filtering can be added if the present implementation does not achieve the desired optimum. What is the design pattern that shapes the existing problem in the statement?
questions.explanation: Visible selected answer received full marks in the Moodle review.

questions.prompt: It is desired to implement the software for an intelligent door camera. It is desired that when someone arrives in front of the door, the camera will notify all the owners of the house that someone has come to the door. The solution allows the addition of new people within the owners who are announced and also the removal of certain owners from this collection. Every time a person passes in front of the door, the viewer has the possibility to realize a photo, to take a 5-second video or to take a 10-second video. The user has the possibility to establish and modify whenever he wants the way in which the viewfinder saves the photos or videos. The photos or videos taken are saved in the internal memory. If the internal memory is fully occupied, they are saved on an SD memory card. When there is no more space on this SD card either, the photos are saved in the cloud. In the cloud it ends up being saved only if there is not enough space in the internal memory and on the SD card, because the costs are high. What are the 3 design patterns that optimally solve these 3 problems ?
questions.explanation: Visible selected answer received full marks in the Moodle review.

questions.prompt: You have created a software application that allows you to broadcast live sports events on different platforms. In the future, you want to be able to add different features to this broadcast dynamically, such as Chat Feature (allows viewers to chat during the broadcast), Viewer Statistics (displays the number of viewers and other statistics in real-time), Advertisements: (inserts advertisements at regular intervals), etc. Choose the proper design pattern and implement a solution that will allow you to extend the collection of features without changing the existing solution. Also, multiple features can be applied to the same initial classic broadcast. The streaming application allows users to issue various actions, such as sending a chat message to another user, start recording the current broadcast, change stream quality/resolution. Each of these operations will be executed asynchronously and will not stop the current live broadcast stream. Choose the proper design pattern and implement a solution that will allow the system to manage a queue of user actions and to be able to process new actions in the future. Select one or more design patterns that will help you implement previous requirements in an efficient manner.
